nodes.speaker: Jason
nodes.text: Hello?

nodes.speaker: Amanda
nodes.text: Hi

nodes.speaker: Max
nodes.text: I see you found this!

nodes.speaker: Max
nodes.text: And who are you?

nodes.speaker: Max
nodes.text: what?

stories.title: Possession
stories.description: When Ash visits his grandmother, he has to fight to survive an evil twisted version of her.

nodes.speaker: Ash
nodes.text: Should I stay?

nodes.speaker: Ashaz
nodes.text: I'm going home!

nodes.speaker: Ashaz
nodes.text: Nah, I'm staying!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hello

nodes.speaker: Max
nodes.text: Why are you running?

nodes.speaker: You

stories.title: The Sidewalk Game
stories.description: What do you think of when you hear the term 'Sidewalk Game'? Hopscotch? Well, it should've been the case. Unfortunately for Ash and Zara, it is a different story.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yo bro! I'm outside your house right now

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Right now? Zara it is 1:00 AM! Why are you even out this late?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I just came back from Donaldson. Semester break has just begun for me!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You came back, just now? This late?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yup!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Anyway, I didn't really expect you to reply to me immediately, so why are you up?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh, I just submitted my final project of this semester. I'm just playing this random horror game I found, though it is missing the 'horror' aspect.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: What is it about?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: The player is tasked to collect 8 random pages in a forest while escaping some tall humanoid figure that's trying to kill them.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Slenderman?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Slenderman.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But honestly, I'm bored. I'm not tired enough to fall asleep.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I can't sleep either. I drank like 4 cups of coffee today.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What? Why?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Idk.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Hey, I have an idea.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: But first of all, do you believe in rituals?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What do you mean? Like, the elevator game? The staircase game?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yes!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Then no. I don't believe in those stories.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: They sound as bogus as the Sadit Jordan and Adriana Smith case.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I agree with you.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: That's why I propose we play 'The Sidewalk Game'

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, now these things are just being made up. 'The Sidewalk Game'???

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What even is that? And how do we play it?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I'm glad you asked! (I was going to tell you anyway)

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: For this game to work, 2 players are needed. This will only work if the players play this game in the times 12:00 AM - 4:30 AM.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: First, both players need to have an object - ideally something that means a lot to them.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Then, the 2 players must be on the opposing sidewalks of the main road. One of the players must be further down the road than the other person.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: To start, both players must say the phrase 'The sidewalk I walk on, the sidewalk I stay on, the sidewalk I move on'. Stupid, I know.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Then both players must step forward exactly 41 times. Ideally, after the 41 steps, both players should be beside the other player's starting point, but instead still on their own sidewalk.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: The players must then place their selected object on the sidewalk with their right hand.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Finally, the players must walk back to their original starting points. HOWEVER, and this is the most important part, the players have to face down, and to NEVER look behind them as they walk back. If the ritual is successful, both players will see the other player's object at their original starting point.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Any questions or comments?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I have a comment - THIS IS PURE STUPIDITY! Please tell me, who would actually believe in such buffoonery like this?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I don't believe it either, trust me, which is why I say we play it anyway, just for the game.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, fine. I guess I'll use my teddy bear plush my childhood friend gave to me before he moved away.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: aww

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Shut up

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What about you? What object are you going to use?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: The bracelet Emily gave me before moving to Australia.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I have not heard of her name in a while. Are you still in contact with her? If so, tell her I said 'hi'.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Thankfully yes, we're both still in contact. I'll send her your message once I get home.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Now, enough chit chat. Let's get this game on the road.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Well, on the sidewalk.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You are sooo funny. You should be a comedian, ya know?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Thank you.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I just realized, your parents' cars aren't in the driveway?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: They went out of the country because my mom is opening a new location of her franchise in Jordan.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Why didn't you go with them?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: My mother was opening the new location during my finals week, so I couldn't really go. They'll be back in like, 4 days.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Alright! Maybe you should throw a party or something once we finish this game.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe I will. Anyway, I'm coming out of the house now.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I see you!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I see you too, though you are on the other sidewalk, so we're gonna have to text to communicate.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Fine by me - your voice is kind of annoying anyway XD

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hey!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I'm just kidding! Or am I?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zara

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Anyway, I guess I'll be the one to be at the lower end of the road.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Alright, I made it!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I didn't think 41 steps would be much, but wow! You went so far, I can't see you anymore even with these street lights!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Good thing we're texting right now, huh?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: For sure. Anyway, you ready to start?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yup, let's start!

nodes.narrator_text: They both say the starting line.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, I just said the line. You?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yes. Let's start counting!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Just made it to 41 steps.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Same. I just placed my item down.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Same. Now remember, once you turn around, do not look back no matter what. Keep your head down, and

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: And I should've told you this earlier, but other versions of this legend tells you to stop moving if you feel breathing down your neck. So do just that. Got it?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Ash...?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Ash you there, man?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Okay, you know what, I'm going to start walking back to my original position. Please text me when you return to your starting spot.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zara

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You texted me at the perfect time - I felt breathing right down my neck as soon as I took 3 steps forward.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm returning back to my starting spot now - 38 steps now. Let me know when you get back.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Ash... what do you see on your sidewalk?

nodes.speaker: Ash
nodes.text: What do you mean? I'm not there yet

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I just have a few more steps until I

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Did you place your bracelet on my driveway?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: No

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I'm also going to assume you didn't place your teddy bear at my starting point?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: So what do the legends say? What happens if the ritual works?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Well, if it works, apparently we get taken to an exact reiplica of our world, except the players would be the only humans there.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: THat's just complete nonsense. You're telling me that by simply saying some simple words, placing an object, and coming back is enough to take you to a different dimension?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Well it seems like it. Notice how no cars are driving by now?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: And before you give me the 'oh because it's like 1:30 in the morning' excuse, remember that even around this time you'd see a car drive by.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ig we'll just wait it out for some time, to see if any car drives by.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: And another piece of evidence to prove my point - you know the wings store that's near your house? You were near there when you reached 41 steps from your starting point.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: It's usually the only store open around this hour. The lights are on, but where's the man who usually works the shift around this hour? I don't see him at the counter like usual.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh, you mean Yusuf? He probably went into the kitchen to prepare an order he likely received over the phone.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I just went in. I rang the bell a couple times. No one's showing up.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Just be patient, He'll proobably be out in a minute.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Yeah uhmm, I'm actually not concerned about that anymore.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh he's out? See? I told you he was just working.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Don't order without me! I could really go for some fries and a veggie burger

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: That's not why I'm not concerned anymore.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I'm looking at the TV right now. It's on the news channel. But, there's no news reporters on the screen, like at all. It's just the background where the anchors usually sit to give news.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: And now I'm also looking at the traffic cameras - there's no cars, on the highways like at all.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Ash, tell me. Did you hear a single car drive by?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, no.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: That means

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: The ritual worked! We're the only humans in this dimension.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, I believe the game now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Idk about you, but I don't want to be here anymore.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Neither do I.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Okay, the good news is, Emily told me everything about this game. I'm just going to scroll back through our chats for a bit to find out how to return to our world.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Emily was the one who told you about this game?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I'm busy, getting the info back, so don't expect a reply from me for a few minutes.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, I'm gonna go explore for a bit.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Okay! I got it!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Basically, we have to again be on opposing sidewalks. Only this time, we have to be on the sidewalk where the other person's object or example, I have to be on the sidewalk where your teddy bear currently is, and you have to be on the sidewalk where my bracelet is.

nodes.narrator_text: Ash and Zara return to their respective sidewalks

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Now, instead of walking this time, we have to run. We have to run 41 steps from our objects, turn around, and run 41 steps back. If we are back in our world, the objects that are meaningful to us should be back at the point we start.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: THis time, we don't have to worry about anybody behind us.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well that's a relief. Alright, I'm running now!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Same!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I can't wait to see cars move in the neighborhood again!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: I just want to see other humans again.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm gonna say hi to Yusuf once we get back, and get the largest order of fries ever known to mankind!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zara?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I came back to my starting point, but instead of seeing my teddy bear, there's blood on your bracelet now.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: And there's blood on your teddy bear.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Did we screw up the ritual?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Okay, let's retrace our steps

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: We both started at opposing sidewalks

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: We both said the statement 'The sidewalk I walk on, the sidewalk I stay on, the sidewalk I move on'

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: We both walked exactly 41 steps in opposing directions, and placed our objects with our right hands

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Wait, stop! You said 'right' hand ...?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: yeah?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Don't tell me you-

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I placed it with my left hand!

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: ASH!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm sorry! I was focused on making 41 steps, I forgot about the hand rule.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zara ... I see something

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: What do you see?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zara, did you change sidewalks?

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: No? Why?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I see, you.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Standing on the same sidewalk as I, staring at me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But you look, creepy. You don't have any eyes. You're covered in blood, and you look more pale than usual.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: Ash, run.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: THAT'S NOT ME

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'M RUNNING FOR MY LIFE! IDK WHERE I'M GOING

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I just got another glance at your clone. Her mouth is completely sewn shut.

nodes.speaker: Zara
nodes.text: wait, what?

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Hey Zara!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Emily, hi! I haven't heard from you in a while. How have you been?

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: I've been doing great! This new job was soooo worth the move. I get to enjoy a beautiful sunset after I finish my shift, and I get paid enough to actually afford necessities.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: We should plan a time for you to visit me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I would love to. I wish I can plan right now, but university has gotten me on a chokehold. I would love to move to Jordan.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Oh yeah! I've been diving deep into urban legends recently. I found out there's a game called 'The Sidewalk Game'

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Sounds silly, yet interesting. Tell me more about it!

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: So the way it works is there needs to be 2 players. This game only works between 12 AM - 4:30 AM in whatever timezone they're in. Both players need to select an object that holds meaning to them. To start the game, both players need to be on the opposing sidewalks of the same road, and one must be further down the street than the other player, 41 steps. Once the other player is at the distance, both players start the game by saying 'The sidewalk I walk on, the sidewalk I stay on, the sidewalk I move on', and then count exactly 41 steps from their starting points.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Once they reach 41 steps, the players place down their selected objects with ideally their right hand as it gives the players a better chance to leave the game than if they did it with their left hand.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Now, this is the most important part: After you place down the item, you must say 'This is the care I own, and am willing to share' BEFORE you turn around.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: After you turn around, DO NOT LOOK BACK. You must count exactly 41 spots from your current spot and try to walk back to your starting point. If at any point you feel breathing down your neck, STOP. Do not move at all! Do not look back either, no matter how scared or curious you are! The breathing will eventually go away.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Once it does, continue where you left off and keep moving to your spawn point.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: If you've done the ritual correctly, your partner's object should be at the spot where you started from.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: The ritual being a success means the players are in an alternate world, where they are the only normal humans. Key word, normal.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: Once the players reach the world, they will only see themselves. No other humans anywhere, no matter, how hard you try.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: They'll be safe for now.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: It's the ritual they have to do afterwards that's risky, because the way the players did the ritual to take them to the world is what affects the possibility of the players successfully returning to the original world.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: To attempt to bring themselves back to the original world, the players must return to the sidewalks where they started from, and run 41 steps away from the object and then run 41 steps back to the object's position.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: If the ritual was successful, the objects the players originally selected will be right in front of them. If not, the objects will not switch, and there will be blood present.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: If the latter happens, it's too late. A disfigured humanoid clone of the player will chase them down with the intention to kill. The ways thures will be disfigured will depend on the mistakes the respective player made in the original.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: For example, let player A be someone who forgot to say one of the lines during the first ritual. Their clone from the other world in result will have their mouth sewn shut.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: And once that clone kills you, your consciousness will transfer to that very clone. Your memories from the other world will be erased, and that clone will take consciousness in your body in the original world.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: WHat?

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: The reason why these clones want to kill is to escape their world, and live in the original world.

nodes.speaker: Emily
nodes.text: So, if you mess up the second ritual, it's game over for you.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: ASH? Are you there???

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: ASH?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ash, I see you!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ash? I see the phone in your hand going off. Why aren't you answering me?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: ... you're not Ash...

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Your eyes are missing...

nodes.speaker: Ash
nodes.text: Look behind you.

nodes.narrator_text: Zara faces eye to eye with herself

stories.title: Roommate
stories.description: Grant moves into his dorm, ready to start his first year of college. What he isn't excited for, was that he was sharing a dorm with 3 other roommates. What makes his story even worse is that one of them has a dark secret.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I finally moved into my dorm!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: How is it?

nodes.speaker: Grant
nodes.text: Surprisingly larger than I expected. Not by much, but I'm satisfied!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: That's great! I heard you're having roommates?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah, 4 excluding me.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What? Dude I feel sorry for you. 2 roommates are already annoying enough (speaking from experience here), but 4?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: A small price to pay for living in a slightly bigger than conventional dormitory. I'd say it's worth it.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, speaking of roommates, have you actually met them?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Only 3 out of the 4, but only because the 4th isn'th here yet. He's actually supposed to come today.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Oh that's cool! What are your roommates like?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, they all seem really nice.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I keep hearing horror stories of irresponsible roommates on the internet.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But Alex (one of the roommates) literally offered to order some takeout for all of us, on him.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: The others seemed really chill. We all had a conversation about what we wanted to do in life.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Man I wish I had your roommates. I only live with one, and that guy refuses to clean up after himself.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: lolololol!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh! The 4th roommate just got here. I'll ttyl!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Alright, later!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hey

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: So ... how is your 4th roommate?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I don't want to be mean, but he seems cold

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Is your dorm cold or something?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No dumbo! It's literally 25 degrees celsius outside right now!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I mean't cold, as in, he seems like an emotionally cold person. Didn't talk much. We all tried to introduce ourselves. He just gave his name, David, and then just went to his room.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Maybe he's just anxious? It is very likely his first year of living outside the comfort of his home, just like yours.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe, but, and I don't want to be too judgy, but his eyes seemed dull. Not as in a 'I miss home' mood, but rather empty, like a void.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Just give him some time. Maybe he'll warm up to you guys eventually.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe. Anyway, I should probably get some sleep now. Classes are going to start tomorrow for me.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Alright man, take care! Good night.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: So how was your first day of university?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Intimidaing. So many people in one lecture room.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Well, people typically drop out after the 3rd semester. If you make it til then (which, let's be honest. you will), the lectures will be less crowded

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I also haven't really seen David today this morning. I've seen everybody else, though.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe he left earlier than usual.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Maybe. Anyway, you wanna hop on Minecraft bedwars around 8:00 ?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Sure!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh, David just came home.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: And he bought a pizza for all of us!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Okay now you're really ticking me off here. Why are you getting awesome roommates, while mine still behaves like a toddler?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Sucks to be you. Anyway, I'm going to consume my pizza.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: 'My'? Like, he bought a pizza for all of you guys INDIVIDUALLY?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yup! Turns out he works at the local pizza store near us. He's allowed to make and take as many free pizzas as he wishes, so each of us get our own WHOLE pizza.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Can we trade roommates?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Nope! Have fun with yours!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I hate you.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: SHADID!

nodes.speaker: YOU
nodes.text: C'MON BRO, PLEASE TELL ME YOU'RE THERE!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I was just about to drift away to sleep, man!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I spent the last 4 hours trying to sleep, and just when I was about to dream, your text woke me up. What could you possibly need at almost 3 in the morning?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I woke up 5 minutes ago to use my restroom.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I go into the dorm lounge. Right as I step outside, I see someone staring at me at the end of the hallway.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I couldn't see who it was, so I walked up to that person.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: As soon as I got close enough, I immediately realized it was David.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I tried to ask why he was just up and standing, but he didn't respond.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Idk why, but I felt colder the closer I approached him.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Grant, there's a good chance he may have been SLEEPwalking.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But his eyes were open!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: More people sleepwalk with their eyes open than closed.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Just don't try to wake him up. He'll eventually return to his bed.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Now, can I please go back to sleep?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yes, sorry for waking you up.
45 PM

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: How did your second day of classes go?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Our class sizes already dropped down by 25%

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: ALREADY? Typically it takes weeks for the class attendance to drop significantly, but dang. Not even 1 week and people are already dropping out.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah, electrical engineering does that for you.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Speaking of majors, do you know what majors your roommates are completing?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, Alex is doing neuroscience, Zareef is doing computer science, and Ivan is studying chemistry.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What about David? Did you not ask him about his major?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh I did. He's actually taking physics.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Sounds fun. A bunch of puzzles to be solved, just like mathematics.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Speaking of David. did you ask him about last night?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I did. He told me he had a dream about riding a horse that travels 200 km/h. That's the only thing he remembers though. Nothing about sleepwalking.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Are you going to tell him about it?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Nah. Too much effort.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Anyway, I gotta dip now. I got a concert to attend.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Alright man, I'll ttyl!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I did not get much sleep at all last night.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Insomnia?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No. It's because of David.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What did he do this time?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He kept humming right outside my door. Well, I think that's what he's been doing.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What do you mean you think?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I tried to tell him to stop humming, but he wouldn't. I went out of my room to confront him, but I didn't find him there. I thought he ran off or something, but that couldn't be the case because the floor outside my door is very creaky. I would've heard him run off if that were the case.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: That's odd. You sure you're not hallucinating or something?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Confident. Anyway, I'm gonna go to classes now. Peace!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Normally, you're the one asking this question. But today's going to be different.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: How was your day today?

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I actually didn't have any classes today, so I just worked out, played Uncharted, and cooked some spaghetti.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You and your simple schedule.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: How about you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, I can't help but notice that our dorm feels a bit colder. Also, Ivan isn't here right now.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Maybe he's staying on campus late tonight?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe, but the weird part was I didn't see him this morning. David hasn't seen him either, and he's usually the one out of all of us who wakes up the earliest.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Maybe he had to do something before classes, so he woke up before David?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe. But on the other hand, David is finally becoming more social.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now he's the guy who starts the conversations at dinner.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He told us about how he grew up in a barn with a single father. He is also really interested in gardening, so hopefully he can one day bringps.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Hey, save me some of those crops, yeah?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: For sure!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Alright, I need to run some errands now, so I'll talk to you later.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, later!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Shadid!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Yo. I'm in the middle of a lecture right now. Shouldn't you be in class right now?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: My only class of the today ended 30 minutes ago, so I'm supposed to be chilling right now.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: 'supposed' ?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Before I left for classes this morning, I went to wish my other roommates a good morning.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But none of them were here.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: So you're scared because you didn't see your roommates this morning who probably already left for classes?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, Ivan still hasn't returned. And don't you think it's kind of awkward how I didn't see any of my other roommates? Surely I'd have seen at least one before I left.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Idk about the others, but perhaps Ivan went to a different town for something and may have forgotten to tell you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I feel like he'd have given us a heads up first.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I should also tell you that while I was trying to sleep last night, I heard what I believe were some muffled screams.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: You probably just imagined that.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Idk dude. Hearing muffled screams and then finding not a single one of my roommates here sounds very skeptical.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Maybe go on a hike or something. I'm confident at least one of them will show up by the time you return.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah maybe I'll do just that.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Be sure to send me some photos of the nature, yeah?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I shall. Anyway, I'm going to let you get back to your lecture.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Grant?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I skipped over something when I checked Alex's room: His backpack is still here.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hold on, Ivan's and Zareef's backpacks are here too!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: In Alex's room?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No, in each of their own room's.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: So then I guess they don't have classes today?

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Or maybe they just came home and then went out again to do something?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: They would've told me otherwise. Something's not adding up.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I have a feeling about something, but I need to do something.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: And that is?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to check David's room. Maybe his bag is there.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, I just entered his room

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Well, is his bag there?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No, actually. His bag isn't here.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Okay, so we can then conclude that David went to a lecture or something.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: As for the others, idk.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, I'm just going to leave the dorm now and go on that much-needed hike.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Huh, that's odd.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What's up?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: There's a book right behind his bookshelf. It's one of those shelves that are completely hollow, so it must've fallen out by accident.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to put it back on the shelf.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Shadid

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I've been here. You can just tell me what's going on.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: <send image>

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: <send image>

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: send <image>

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: send <image>

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: What the hell?

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: BRO GET OUT OF THERE. YOU NEED TO CALL THE COPS!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: HE JUST ENTERED THE DORM. I CAN'T LEAVE OR HE'S GOING TO KNOW THAT I SNOOPED IN HIS ROOM!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You know what, I'm going to think about this later. I'm going to go on a hike.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: That's the spirit! Now please let me focus on my lecture.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I will. Later!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You were right, Shadid. I really needed this hike.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Told ya! Any cool rivers you're about to walk by?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Just half a kilometer away. I'll probably stop there to have my lunch.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Which is ...?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Pasta with ground chicken, and parmesan sauce. And also a cup of yogurt with frozen blueberries.

nodes.speaker: Shadi
nodes.text: Okay, I see you healthy guy!

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Shadid?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Yo bro, where are you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I could ask you the same thing! I haven't seen you this morning.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: my bad. I had to attend an event.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Now, where are you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm on a hike. Why?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Just curious. Anyway dude, I got some free food from the event. I'll have some ready on the table for you when you get back.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ooh, exciting! What'd you bring?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Chicken kebab wraps, with extra garlic sauce and hummus on the side.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: YO THAT SOUNDS SO GOOD. YOU KNOW WHAT, I'M COMING BACK RIGHT NOW!

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Alright! I'm just gonna heat it up now since it's kind of cold.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: grant

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ivan! Where have you been for the past 2 days?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Dude I got kidnapped.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Not just me, Alex and Zareef too.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zareef?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Yeah, Zareef.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But I was just talking to him! He said he just came back from an event and had some food ready for me.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Grant, that's NOT ZAREEF

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: THAT'S DAVID!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: what???

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Yesterday, he kidnapped me and kept me in some barn, which was why you didn't see me since then.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Today, he brought both Alex and Zareef over. Now he' coming for you!

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Whatever you do, DO NOT GO BACK TO THE DORM.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hang on, how do I know you're not pranking me?

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Hello, Grant?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hi Monica, what's up!

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Have you heard from Grant since yesterday?

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: We tried to call him last night, but he didn't pick up. We tried to call him again this morning, but no answer. It's not like Ivan to avoid picking up calls from his family.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I haven't actually. I thought he might've gone back to you guys because of some type of family emergency, so I assumed he just forgot to let me know.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'll try to look for him over here. Meanwhile, you should file a missing person's report.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Okay, thank you! We're so worried!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I am too. But hopefully we'll find him.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Alright, I'm gonna ask his other roommates if they've seen him before I file the report.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, Monica, I haven't been completely honest with you.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: It is true that I haven't seen Ivan since yesterday. The truth is, one of our roommates kidnapped him.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: WHAT?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: and he kidnapped my other roommates today. He's coming for me next!

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Okay okay hold on right there - why didn't you just tell me everything from the beginning?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Because right before you texted me, I got a text from Ivan

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He told me that he and my other roommates were kidnapped by David.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: They're currently in some barn.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Ivan warned me not to go back to the dorm because David is apparently waiting for me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I wasn't sure if he was playing some sick joke or not until you told me you haven't heard from him.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I wanted to wait for more of your text messages before I told you the truth.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: I see. Okay, so what are you going to do now?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Let me just...

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Huh? Grant why'd you add me to this group?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'll explain later, but Ivan, I believe you.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Ivan?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Monica!

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Is it true? You've been kidnapped?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: Yeah, by David!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: WAIT OKAY HANG ON. SOMEONE LET ME KNOW WHAT'S GOING ON!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Long story short, David kidnapped most of my roommates and placed them in some barn. Now, he's returned and is waiting for me next at the dorm.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: So that hike really did save you.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Wait, Ivan, how are you texting us if you got kidnapped?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: I had my phone in my pockets when David came after me.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: I usually sleep with jeans on, so I'm guessing David didn't really suspect I'd have my phone with me.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I'm sorry, you sleep with jeans on? Grant what are your roommates?

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Now's not the time for such comments.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Sorry.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Grant, do you know where you are, specifically?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: I might have an idea. But just to confirm, we're going to look around together.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I might have an idea too, actually.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Grant, remember when you told me about David's backstory? Which town did he grow up in? Did he mention that?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He said he grew up in Shadowroove.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Okay, I'm just gonna conduct some quick searches.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Grant, do you know David's full name?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He said his last name is Rosskick, but if he was smart, that's probably a fake name. His name probably isn't even David either.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Anyway, you guys look into whatever you can/ I'm gonna try to stall 'Zareef'.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hey Zareef, what was the event that you attended earlier?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: There was an intro meeting for the astrophysics organization today.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: mmm, that sounds fascinating.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, I'm going to walk back now.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: You okay?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: WHat?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: You seem less excited. Before you went silent, you seemed soo ecstatic to eat the food I've brought.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh I still am! I'm just tired right now because of all the hiking.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Just leave the food on the table, I'll be back in around 30 minutes.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: 30 minutes??

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I've walked quite far

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright I'm ba

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Who changed the name?

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: I think you already know who

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: wow, anyway. I told David I'd be back in 30 minutes. Did you guys find anything?

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: All of us feel stronger now.

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: That's great to hear.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: We just opened the barn door. There's sunlight!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Let's go! But still be careful - he might have some affiliates or something.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: In the meantime, can you guys describe what you see? I'm currently looking at a barn on Google Maps. I strongly believe it's the same barn you guys were placed in.

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: There's a lot of wheat growing in front of us. Just a few hundred meters down the road that's connected to the barn is a gray 1-story house.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Is there a sign nearby that says 'Welcome to Shadowroove'

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: No, I don't see

nodes.speaker: Ivan
nodes.text: WAIT NVM I DO!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: OH MY GOODNESS I FOUND Y'ALL!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now's not the time to celebrate. Monica, can you call emeregency services and send them to rescue Ivan and the others?

nodes.speaker: Monica
nodes.text: Already on it!

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: I already called the cops to your dorm, Grant. They said they'll be there in 10 minutes.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright cool!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to stall David a little more.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Actually Zareef, I changed my mind. You can just go ahead and store the food in the fridge. I'll heat it up for later.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Very well then.

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: How are you enjoying the Thomasgrove river?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: It's actually kind of beautiful

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Zareef, how did you know I was at the Thomasgrove river, specifically?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Cut the nonsense, Grant. I know you know it's me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What did you to the others?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Oh nothing. Just preparing them for a little, sacrifice.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: SACRIFICE?

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: Yes. Had you've been in the dorm, I would've won!

nodes.speaker: Zareef
nodes.text: But it's no problem - you're in my line of sight anyway.

nodes.narrator_text: You hear some leaves rustling behind you. You immediately try to run out of the trail. You were successful. Then you proceeded to run back to the dorm, where the cops have been waiting. Foolishly for David, he was right behind you with a knife when the cops saw him. He was immediately tackled down and arrested on the spot. David Rosskick (real last name being Groovecutter) was arrested for kidnapping Ivan Slandi, Alex Morashell, and and Zareef Sujood, attemptedmurder and attempted kidnapping of Grant Miller. He has since been sentenced to life in prison. All of his kidnapping victims were found and rescued from his family barn located in Shadowgroove.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm hiding in his closet.

nodes.narrator_text: Bad move! David walks into the room, and hears you in the closet. You lost - try again.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to confront him.

nodes.speaker: Shadid
nodes.text: Dude don't!

nodes.narrator_text: Before you could even open your mouth, David suffocates you with a plastic bag. When you wake up, you notice him performing the ritual.

stories.title: Night Shift
stories.description: Desperate, Jayne decides to take the night shift at her local cafe. Coffees, pastries, and less customers? What could go wrong?

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Welcome to your first shift at the cafe, Jane. We're glad to have you on board!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Thank you for this opportunity!

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: No, thank you! It is understandably hard to get someone to work on the night shift.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Now, here is some information about your job.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Your shifts start from 11 PM - 7 AM from Monday to Friday.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Because you are on the night shift, you are allowed to be on your phone during the job, as long as you're serving the customers.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: You have the coffee machine. If you ever run out of coffee grounds, you can get some more from the back of the store. You won't have to open the coffee filter every time after each use as the machine has a little monitor indicating how much grounds are left.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: As for the desserts, you're only responsible for baking the cookies. Everything else, like the cake, brownies, and muffins, you don't have to bake them, since you're new to this kind of job. Remember to put oven mittens on before you put or take anything out of the oven.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Once again, you can find the cookie dough in back beside the coffee grounds.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: And one more thing, you're going to experience some 'unusual' customers during these night shifts.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: It's important that you give them exactly what they want.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: The good news is, with the phone you're currently receiving these instructions on, these 'customers' will text their order. They will be expecting their whole order to be ready by the time they walk in.

nodes.speaker: Manager
nodes.text: Just give what they request, and you'll be good for the shift. Good luck, and text me if you need anything!

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Hey, how's your first shift going?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: It's actually kind of peaceful. Barely any customers around this time, and I get double the pay of the day shift workers.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: This was also the only job I was able to get during these times, but I'm grateful.

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Cool!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Anyway, why are you up around this time?

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Group project.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Did you finally get a group where you're not the only one putting in work?

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: If I did, I would've been sleeping right now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Dang, that's tough.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh! First customer of the shift. I'll ttyl!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Bro! I just got a $10 tip!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: The customer's coffee was only like $4 though.

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Okay I see you! You must be vibrating some great energy for that tip!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe working here was the best choice I got

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hold on this is odd. There's suddenly more customers coming in, like 8. It's only 11:30 right now.

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Well, don't keep them waiting! Get to work!

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Hello. I'd like to place an order for a chocolate chip cookie and a small maple latte.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Who are you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hello?

nodes.narrator_text: You have a line of 5 people. Do you take the next person's order or do you fulfill the unknown's order?

choices.text: Start working on the unknown's order

nodes.narrator_text: You start working on the unknown person's order. As a result, 3 customers left the shop. You then take the remaining 2 people's orders, and  they go on with their nights. Just when you thought you could relax, you hear the door open. Stepping in is a tall (at least 7 ft 7) human. You can't see their face. They point towards the food you have prepared for them. You hand them their food and move on with your shift.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Some really tall dude, I'm talking like at least 7 ft, walked into the store. He was eerily quiet, but thankfully I prepared his order ahead of time.

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: 7 ft? Dang that's really tall! Anyway, I presume you've just finished serving the customers who suddenly came in?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yup! How's your group project going?

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: Don't ask. All you have to know is, it's going horrendous.

nodes.speaker: Raiyan
nodes.text: My teammates keep missing our planned meetings for this thing. It feels like me and this other guy are the only ones who are actually trying to pass the course!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Man that's rough. Yeah group projects are always risky becuase you never know your teammate's work ethics (even they even have those) until it's too late

stories.title: Possession
stories.description: When Omar notices that his mother has been acting off recently, he realizes he has a long battle to fight.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Dad?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Where are you?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I'm still at work. Why?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Grandma has been acting weird ever since I got here.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: How so?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: When you left, her smile disappeared. Usually she's happy to see me. But today she seemed cold.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Later she gave me lunch. I thought she was going to eat with me today like usual, but instead she told me she already ate.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now I hear whispers coming from her room. Did I do something wrong?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: No, you didn't do anything wrong.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Gradnma is just a bit sick, that's all.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: But whatever you do, please don't go into her room, no matter what.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I don't want you to get sick.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: If she's sick, why did you even send me to stay with her?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I'm sorry, young one. I'd normally hire a babysitter for you, but right now times are tough.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: And the last time I left you to be home alone, you almost burnt the house down by just boiling a pot of water. I don't know how you even managed to do that to this day.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Anyway, I actually just finished my shift, so I'm coming to pick you up now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, please hurry. Grandma's starting to act even more weird now.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I'm using speech to text right now to talk with you. What is Grandma doing now?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I keep hearing scratching from her bedroom door. And the whispers from her room are getting louder.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Okay, Emily, I need you to listen to me very carefully.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: The person in Grandma's room right now is NOT her. I repeat, she is NOT your Grandma now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What???

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: That makes no sense. I saw her physically go into her room and lock herself in there.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: And I'm hearing her voice right now.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: What is she saying?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: She's asking me to bring her a glass of water.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily, DO NOT BRING HER THE WATER.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: But she's thirsty? Why would I not give her water?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Because like I said, right now, that's not your Grandma.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: More importantly, Emily, I need you to hide somewhere. Please! If you do, I'll let you stay home alone from now on.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily are you okay?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm fine. I'm currently hiding in the kitchen pantry.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Look for anything to defend yourself with, if you can.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I have a baseball bat. Since when did Grandma play baseball?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: She never played baseball. You have my bat from when I used to play baseball.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: But enough about my past, do you hear anything right now?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Actually, no. As I was moving to the kitchen, the scratching stopped. I don't hear her voice, anymore.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Okay. Don't leave the pantry yet, I'm almost here.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: DAD

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: GRANDMA'S SCREAMING RIGHT NOW

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: EMILY DO NOT GO TO HER

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: BUT SHE'S IN PAIN. I HEAR HER CRYING.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: YOU KEEP SAYING THAT'S NOT GRANDMA. WHAT IS GOING ON? YOU'RE NOT TELLING ME ANYTHING.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I'LL TELL YOU AFTER I PICK YOU UP. PLEASE, DO NOT GO TO HER. I PROMISE YOU THAT IS NOT HER!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to help her! You're making no sense!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: EMILY NO!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: EMILY

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: EMILY!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Emily can't come to the phone right now

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: LEAVE MY CHILD ALONE, YOU DEMON!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Oh she's left alone all right.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: What did you do to her?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I just put her to sleep, temporarily. She'll wake up soon, but by then your mother will be gone!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Oh no you don't!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: DAD!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I knocked the thing out.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: That's great! I'm in the driveway! Can you run to the car?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Already on my way!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I see you

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: ... You're not Emily, aren't you

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: NOPE!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm staying here.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Okay, good!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Just give me 2-3 minutes, Emily. I'm nearly there.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You have to tell me what's going on though.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I don't even know where to begin.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Just try!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Alright, here I go.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Your Grandma was the only child of a member who was a part of a cult back a few decades ago.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: As soon as your grandma was born, your great grandparents did everything in their power to leave the cult.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: They were almost successful, actually, until the leader found out.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: To punish your great grandparents, the cult kidnapped your grandma and performed a ritual on her. This ritual would result in her being possessed by a demon every few random years. Your great grandparents did everything they could in their power to stop the demon from coming back, but nothing worked.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Despite all this, grandma managed to live a normal life. She met your grandpa, he found out about the situation, but still decided to propose to her anyway.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I haven't really told you the truth about Grandpa's death, Emily.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: He didn't die in his sleep from natural causes.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: On the night of his death, grandma had a nother posession episode. Grandpa thought he was prepared though. He tried to perform an exorcism on her. Unlike the other attempts of an exorcism on her done by her parents, this one actually succeeded. Or, that's what we both thought.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: We all went to sleep. It was around 1 in the morning when I heard your Grandpa scream.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I rushed into the bedroom, and saw your grandma, standing over your grandpa.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Only, it wasn't her. Again, it was the demon.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I tried to save him, but the demon kept using his dark magic to prevent me from stepping closer into the room.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: In the end, the demon killed him, and grandma fell on the floor unconscious.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: AND YOU THOUGHT IT'D BE BETTER TO BRING ME HERE AND RISK MY LIFE THAN TO SIMPLY JUST RISK THE HOUSE BEING BURNED DOWN????

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: WHAT IS ACTUALLY WRONG WITH YOU? ARE YOU EVEN MY FATHER?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily, please, I'm sorry!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: She had another posession episode last month, so I thought it'd be safe to bring you here.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I thought it was only once every couple years.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I'll take you to your favorite ice cream store after this. I just got into the driveway.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Please, do not come out just yet.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: How are you going to get in?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Spare key.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay, but be careful.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I heard the door open.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Wait, you're already here? I hear breathing outside the pantry.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily, I'm in the hallway right now. That's not me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: SHE'S TWISTING THE DOORKNOB. DAD HELP!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Dad?

nodes.narrator_text: Emily's father managed to successfully knock out 'Grandma'. Emily was then rescued. A week after this incident, Emily and her father moved out of town to get away from her grandma. They ended up living with the father's friend. However, her grandma was shortly reported missing after that. The police checked the household, but found no signs of her.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Dad I got into the engineering program!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: I knew you could do it! I'm so proud of you!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Not only that, but they also gave me a full-ride entry scholarship!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: That's my daughter! How about we go out to that Lebanese restaurant to celebrate?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Wait, you're home already?

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: What? No? I'm still at work.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe it's John who's making all those footsteps downstairs.

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: Emily, hide now!

nodes.speaker: Dad
nodes.text: John went out of town to go to his sister's wedding

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What?

nodes.narrator_text: Emily hears a familiar voice call her name

stories.title: Doppelganger
stories.description: Sofia always thought she had a normal family. That was, until a certain somebody texted her.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Camping trip is in 5 days! You excited?

nodes.speaker: Allie
nodes.text: Absolutely! This is the first time my parents are letting me go out of town without them!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, we just finished university, so we lowkey could use this trip.

nodes.speaker: Allie
nodes.text: Agreed. Make sure you bring the fishing rods! You keep bragging about how many fish you catch every time you go fishing.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I must have the 'Luck Of The Sea' enchantment in real life! My last trip I caught like 15 basses in 2 hours. I caught so much that when I was on my 10th fish, my father asked if he could attempt to reel the fish in.

nodes.speaker: Allie
nodes.text: Souds like this trip is going to be promising then. I already got my fishing license, so we should be good.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hang on, I just heard a knock on my door. I'll brb!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: THE HELL?!?

nodes.speaker: Allie
nodes.text: Sofia? What's going on?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Hello, Sofia

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I think you may have the wrong number

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: I'm pretty confident you're my intended contact, Sofia Garcia.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Who are you? How do you know my full name?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: If I were to tell you, you would not believe me.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: But it's okay, I'm going to tell you anyway. I'm your sister.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What are you talking about? I don't have a sister.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Of course they hid me from you. Your parents kept me hidden from you. You were never an only child like they made you think.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Quit playing this sick prank on me, whoever you are. Leave me alone!

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: You can try to ignore this, but it's not going to work.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: I knew you weren't going to believe me. Thankfully, the evidence to my claims are actually in your house right now.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Your room has a green carpet, you have a Minecraft poster hanging above your bed, and you also have that teddy bear your mother gave you when you were 4.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Why do you know all of this information?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: That's not important. Now, I'd like you to get off your couch and head to your parents room.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Why?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Because my proof lies in your parents bedroom.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Okay.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright I'm here.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Now go under your parents bed.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I see a bunch of coins underneath the bed.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: How are these coins proof that I have a sister?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: They're not. Take one of the coins underneath the bed and look at it. Do they look like normal coins to you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Actually no. They don't have anything on it, actually, besides the ridges.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Perfect. Now go to the piggy bank on the tablestand right next to the bookshelf, and insert the coin.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Doing that right now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: The bookshelf is moving.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: What the- there's a secret room here.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: With a safe on a stand?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah!

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Inside the safe lies the evidence that I exist as your sibling.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: You seem to know about the interior of my house a little too well, including a secret room that I was not aware of.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to assume you know where the key is.

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Actualy, no. I don't know where the key is. I know that it is a red key with 3 'a''s back-to-back.

nodes.narrator_text: Sofia hears the front door getting unlocked.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: My parents are here! What do I do?

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Don't panic. Get out of the room, tap the piggy bank's nose to move the bookshelf right back into it's original place, and get out of the room!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Done and dusted!

nodes.speaker: Unknown
nodes.text: Now, whatever you do, do NOT tell your parents any of this. I'll text you tomorrow. Good night!

stories.title: Babysitting
stories.description: Kate has always babysat the Greenspoon's child. This time, Kate is not aware of what she was about to face.

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: Hello Kate

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Hi Mr. Greenspoon!

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: My wife and I are planning to go out this Saturday night. I was wondering if you'd be able to babysit Jackson from 9 PM to 1 AM?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yes, I'm available for that time that day!

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: Okay, thank you! I'll see you then!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Hey Kate, are you down to get some milkshakes this Saturday night?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'd like to, but I can't. I told Mr. Greenspoon that I'd babysit his child that day.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Aw man.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: At least you'll be paid a lot, because he's rich.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Actually, now that I think about it, how do the Greenspoons have so much money?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I actually don't know. But I don't really care. As long as I'm getting paid, I have no reason to care.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Sounds a bit harsh reading that from you.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I don't really care. Anyway, I gotta go eat dinner now. I'll talk to you soon!

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Kate, are you sure you can't join us for milkshakes tonight?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm already near the Greenspoon's residence. Even if I weren't, I already told Mr. Greenspoon I'd babysit Jackson. On the bright side, I'm going to be making like $400 when I finish this shift.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Let her get that money, Anna. Plus, who knows? Maybe Kate will buy us those leather jackets we wanted?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Not a chance, Joseph

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: It was worth a shot.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Hey guys, I just woke up! What'd I miss?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Mike, it's 8:45 PM. What time did you sleep?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Actually, don't answer that. I don't want to even comprehend.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: You guys are getting milkshakes? Without me?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: I tried calling you like 7 times 30 minutes ago. You're a very deep sleeper!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Oooh - I see the missed calls.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Kate's not going either because of a babysitting gig.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Hey Kate, split some of that money with us, would you?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: I already tried, but she rejected the idea.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Understandable.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: So the rest are still going?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Yup!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Alright, I'll meet y'all at the Diner. It's gonna take a while for me to get ready.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Just got to the Greenspoon's driveway. Maybe once Jackson goes to sleep, I can text y'all.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Sounds like a great plan!

nodes.speaker: Kate
nodes.text: Mr. Greenspoon, I'm here.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I don't see your car at all?

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: Hi Kate. I apologize, but my wife and I messed up our dinner reservations. I accidentally selected 8:45 instead.

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: I left a spare key under the mat. Jackson's already asleep, so feel free to make yourself something to eat.

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: We'll still be back around 1 in the morning. If there's an emergency, don't hesitate to contact me.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Alright, thank you!

nodes.speaker: Mr. Greenspoon
nodes.text: I'm sorry.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: It's alright. We all make mistakes, sometimes.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well that was weird.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: What happened?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Mr Greenspoon already left the house before I got there. He said he messed up his reservations.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Normally he'd wait for me to enter his house and give me instructions in-person.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Huh, that is weird.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: So your gig got cancelled?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No - he left a spare key, and told me to watch over Jackson.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Aw, I was getting excited thinking we were going to enjoy a night out as a group again.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, I'm going to be free tomorrow, if y'all want to do something.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: I say we go to a haunted house attraction! I can't wait to see Joseph get so scared he throws up!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Don't remind me

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Oh those were some really funny times. But yeah, I'm down for that!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Same, and this time I'm going to be brave!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm down too!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Anyway, since Jackson's sleeping, I can text y'all on the job!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: You're not gonna check on him?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now that you mention it, I probably should. Just to make sure he's sleeping, and if not, well he'll now know that his favorite babysitter is here!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: I thought you only wanted the money.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well sometimes as a babysitter, you tend to bond with the kids whom you babysit. Jackson's no exception over here.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Enough chatting, I'm gonna check on him.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: While she's doing that, I just got a table for us at the diner.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: How far out are you guys right now?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: I'm like 5 minutes away. Mike?

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: I'm currently in the shower.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: sorry

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: WHAT?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Like, you have your phone with you right now, while you're showering?

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: yeah?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: I'm not surprised. Just don't stay too long in the shower.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: No need to. I just got out. I'll get to the diner in 15 minutes.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Guys

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I just checked on him. He seems to be in a deep sleep. I'm not going to wake him up.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Also Mike, you really need to learn when to not be on your phone. No sane person brings a phone into the shower!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: And continue missing out on these conversations? No way!

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Wow, you're hopeless.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: The diner is awfully quiet, tonight.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Really?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Yeah there's like, no customers at all.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Actually, I just realized, I'm the only customer here.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Ooh, spooky!

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Shut up, Mike! Jane and the other staff are still here, thankfully.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: And they're playing Yiruma over the speakers! Now this is the vibe I love to enjoy!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: I'm almost there, Anna!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now y'all are making me regret not coming with y'all.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Hey, we still have that haunted house gig tomorrow.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Yeah, I'm gonna be looking forward to Joseph's reaction!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Wow

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Well, while you guys enjoy your milkshakes, I'm gonna make myself a sandwich.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: And Joseph's here!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Guys she wasn't kidding. It's quiet here.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: But I missed the Yiruma music, so now they're playing Shawn Mendes

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: I just left the house. Someone start the 15 minute timer now!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Started the timer! If we don't see you when the timer runs out, you're going to be mixing ketchup and mustard into your milkshake.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: And if I get there beforehand, BOTH YOU and Anna are going to do just that!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Deal!

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: I did not agree to this!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Too bad! Now you're in on the bet!

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Hey Kate, will Mr. Greenspoon allow me to visit you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Sorry Anna, but it seems like you're on your own!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Guys?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: What's up?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: There's someone outside the front door

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: He's wearing a creepy mask

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: HE'S GOT A KNIFE

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: WHAT?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: CALL 911!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: I just left the house. I'm coming over, Kate!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Me and Anna are also on our way!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm on the phone with the operator.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: They said the police will get here in 15 minutes

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: You think you can hold the guy off for 15 minutes? Actually, is he doing anything else?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: No. Matter of fact, he's not at the door anymore.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Maybe it's some guy playing a sick prank? It's almost Halloween after all.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: We're still coming over. Better safe than sorry.

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Mike, your house is the closest out of all of us to the Greenspoon's residence. How far away are you from it?

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: I'm 5 minutes away. I should be able to get here before the cops.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I got a knife, just in case.

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Good!

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: Kate, check in on Jackson.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Will do.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Now that I think of it, Jackson's room is horrifyingly close to the entrance.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Take him out of there, and find somewhere safer to hide.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'll hide in the attic. Heading over to his room right now.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: uhm, guys

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: <send image>

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: huh?

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: What?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: The hell?

nodes.speaker: Joseph
nodes.text: That explains his wealth.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Kate I'm here! I don't see the masked guy anywhere.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Where are you guys?

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: We're 5 minutes away.

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: HE'S IN THE HOUSE!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: WHERE?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I DON'T KNOW, BUT ALL I HEAR ARE FOOTSTEPS UPSTAIRS!

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: HANG ON KATE

nodes.speaker: Anna
nodes.text: Kate, where are you?

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Still in Jackson's room!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm hiding under his bed

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: I'm at the front door. Kate, you said Jackson's bedroom is really close to the main door. Do you think you'd be able to rush there and let me in, or is it too risky?

choices.text: Rush to the door to let Mike in

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: Stand by, Mike. I'm going to let you in.

nodes.speaker: Mike
nodes.text: Roger that!

nodes.speaker: You
nodes.text: I'm going to stay here

stories.title: What's wrong with my future?
stories.description: Justin woke up from a weird dream, only to later realize that it was not a dream.

stories.title: Can't sleep
stories.description: Shayne has been suffering from insomnia. His days are only about to get worse.

stories.title: Ex-friend
stories.description: Many times, friendships come to an end. Many times, it's a painful ending with anger. For Pedro, anger would've been a way better ending than whatever he has to deal with it.

stories.title: The Man With The White Sweater
The Woman In The Wheel Chair
